title: Avoid including linux/memfd.h
body:
The file is not available on older distros.

xref:  #1600, containers/skopeo#643

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
Looks like ubuntu is not too happy.
@epruesse Can we do something to support Ubuntu?

Yes.... This was just an attempt. I did not have time to research much.

According to `man memfd_create`, the interface was added with kernel 3.17 and glibc support with 2.27. What exactly does `unshare.c` do actually? Since the interface is linux specific, it won't work on e.g. OSX, so it can't be required. Is it possible to simply disable building it when `memfd.h` is not found?

The memfd is used to fix a potential vulnerability where the container can get access to the host via `/proc/self/exe`.  Not sure what is the way to check for its presence without something like autoconf.  In any case, `unshare.c` is specific to Linux so portability doesn't matter.  If we want to drop it, it is enough I think, to define the missing `MFD_ALLOW_SEALING` and `MFD_CLOEXEC`
I agre lets define those if they are not defined after inporting the mman.h file.
@epruesse Is this still something you are interested in?

@epruesse I will take this over.

Replaced by  #1655
